metadata.description: Templatable command patterns extracted from existing templates

template.content:
Start the {FRAMEWORK} development server with hot-reload enabled.

Arguments:
- $1: Port number (optional, defaults to {DEFAULT_PORT})
- $2: Host address (optional, defaults to localhost)

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Start on default port ({DEFAULT_PORT})
- `/{COMMAND} 8080` - Start on custom port
- `/{COMMAND} 8080 0.0.0.0` - Start on custom port and host

This will:
1. Check if port is available
2. Start development server with hot-reload
3. Display access URL
4. Watch for file changes

Press Ctrl+C to stop the server.

Execute the {FRAMEWORK} test suite using {TEST_FRAMEWORK}.

Arguments:
- $1: Specific test file/directory/pattern (optional, runs all tests if not specified)
- $2: Additional flags like --watch, --coverage, --verbose

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Run all tests
- `/{COMMAND} {TEST_PATH_EXAMPLE}` - Run specific tests
- `/{COMMAND} --coverage` - Run tests with coverage report
- `/{COMMAND} --watch` - Run tests in watch mode

This will:
1. Discover and run tests
2. Display results with pass/fail status
3. Show coverage report (if --coverage flag used)
4. Exit with appropriate status code

Test files should be named {TEST_FILE_PATTERN}.

Check code quality using {LINTER}.

Arguments:
- $1: Specific path to lint (optional, lints all code if not specified)
- --fix: Automatically fix issues where possible

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Check all code
- `/{COMMAND} {PATH_EXAMPLE}` - Check specific path
- `/{COMMAND} --fix` - Check and auto-fix issues

This will:
1. Scan code for style violations
2. Report issues with file locations
3. Auto-fix fixable issues (if --fix used)
4. Exit with non-zero code if issues found

Configuration: {LINTER_CONFIG_FILE}

Automatically format code using {FORMATTER}.

Arguments:
- $1: Specific path to format (optional, formats all code if not specified)

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Format all code
- `/{COMMAND} {PATH_EXAMPLE}` - Format specific path

This will:
1. Format code according to style guide
2. Update files in-place
3. Report which files were modified

Configuration: {FORMATTER_CONFIG_FILE}

Note: This command modifies files directly.

Perform static type checking using {TYPE_CHECKER}.

Arguments:
- $1: Specific path to check (optional, checks all code if not specified)

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Check all code
- `/{COMMAND} {PATH_EXAMPLE}` - Check specific path

This will:
1. Analyze type annotations
2. Report type errors and inconsistencies
3. Suggest type improvements
4. Exit with non-zero code if errors found

Configuration: {TYPE_CHECKER_CONFIG_FILE}

Create optimized production build of the {FRAMEWORK} application.

Arguments:
- $1: Build mode (optional, defaults to production)

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Build for production
- `/{COMMAND} --mode development` - Build for development

This will:
1. Clean previous build artifacts
2. Bundle and optimize code
3. Generate production assets
4. Create source maps (if configured)
5. Output build statistics

Output directory: {BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}

After building, you can:
- Test with `/{PREVIEW_COMMAND}` (if available)
- Deploy to production
- Analyze bundle size

Generate a new {COMPONENT_TYPE} with proper structure and boilerplate.

Arguments:
- $1: Name of the {COMPONENT_TYPE} (required)
- $2: Additional options (optional, e.g., --type, --path)

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND} {NAME_EXAMPLE}` - Create basic {COMPONENT_TYPE}
- `/{COMMAND} {NAME_EXAMPLE} {OPTION_EXAMPLE}` - Create with options

This will:
1. Validate the name format
2. Check if {COMPONENT_TYPE} already exists
3. Generate {FILE_TYPES} with boilerplate
4. Add imports/exports where needed
5. Display created files and next steps

Generated files:
- {FILE_PATTERN}

Naming conventions:
- {NAMING_CONVENTION}

After creation:
- {POST_CREATION_STEPS}

Start an interactive {FRAMEWORK} shell with project context loaded.

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Start interactive shell

This will:
1. Load {FRAMEWORK} configuration
2. Import common modules
3. Provide access to {AVAILABLE_OBJECTS}
4. Start interactive REPL

Useful for:
- Testing code snippets
- Inspecting database
- Running quick experiments
- Debugging

Pre-loaded:
{PRELOADED_ITEMS}

Type `exit()` or press Ctrl+D to exit.

Deploy the {FRAMEWORK} application to {PLATFORM}.

Arguments:
- $1: Target environment (optional, defaults to {DEFAULT_ENV})

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Deploy to {DEFAULT_ENV}
- `/{COMMAND} {ENV_EXAMPLE}` - Deploy to specific environment

This will:
1. Verify deployment prerequisites
2. Run tests (if configured)
3. Build production assets (if needed)
4. Upload to {PLATFORM}
5. Run post-deployment tasks
6. Display deployment URL

Prerequisites:
{DEPLOYMENT_PREREQUISITES}

Environment variables needed:
{REQUIRED_ENV_VARS}

After deployment:
- Verify application is running
- Check logs for errors
- Run smoke tests

Install or update project dependencies using {PACKAGE_MANAGER}.

Arguments:
- $1: Specific package to install (optional, installs all if not specified)
- $2: Version constraint (optional)

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Install all dependencies
- `/{COMMAND} {PACKAGE_EXAMPLE}` - Install specific package
- `/{COMMAND} {PACKAGE_EXAMPLE}@{VERSION}` - Install specific version

This will:
1. Read {DEPENDENCY_FILE}
2. Install packages with {PACKAGE_MANAGER}
3. Update {LOCK_FILE}
4. Display installation summary

After installation:
- Verify imports work
- Update .gitignore if needed
- Document new dependencies in README

Start local server to preview production build.

Arguments:
- $1: Port number (optional, defaults to {DEFAULT_PORT})

Usage:
- `/{COMMAND}` - Preview on default port
- `/{COMMAND} 8080` - Preview on custom port

This will:
1. Verify build exists (run /{BUILD_COMMAND} if needed)
2. Start production server
3. Serve optimized assets
4. Display preview URL

This preview simulates production but:
- Uses local server instead of production infrastructure
- May not include production optimizations (CDN, etc.)
- Useful for final testing before deployment

Press Ctrl+C to stop the server.

notes.usage: To use these templates, replace placeholders like {FRAMEWORK}, {COMMAND}, {PORT} with actual values for your framework.
placeholders: {COMPONENT_TYPE} - Type of component (model, component, blueprint, etc.)
notes.customization: Adapt the content to match your framework's conventions, tools, and best practices. Not all commands apply to all frameworks.
